title: Payroll portal password reset
scenario_text: A user receives an email claiming their payroll portal password will expire today unless they reset it immediately using a provided link.
why_someone_might_trust_it: Payroll access is important and the message sounds routine.
recommended_action: Do not click the link. Open the payroll portal directly from a known bookmark or official site and report the message.

title: Fake parcel delivery fee
scenario_text: A message claims a parcel is delayed and asks the user to pay a small delivery fee through a link.
attacker_goal: Capture payment details and personal information.
why_someone_might_trust_it: Many people are waiting for deliveries and the fee seems small.
recommended_action: Do not use the link. Check the parcel status through the courier's official website or app.

title: Manager asks for gift cards
scenario_text: An email appears to come from a manager asking the employee to urgently buy gift cards for a client meeting and send back the codes.
attacker_goal: Obtain money through gift card fraud.
why_someone_might_trust_it: Employees may fear delaying a manager's request.
recommended_action: Verify through a known internal channel before taking any action and report the message.

title: Cloud storage shared document alert
scenario_text: A user receives a shared-document notification that asks them to log in to view an urgent document.
why_someone_might_trust_it: Shared document alerts are common in workplaces.
recommended_action: Do not use the embedded link. Access the cloud platform directly and check whether the document is real.

title: Fake telecom account protection call
scenario_text: A caller claims to be from a telecom provider and says unusual activity is occurring on the user's mobile account. They pressure the user to confirm personal details immediately.
attacker_goal: Collect personal details and bypass account verification.
red_flags: pressure to stay on the line
why_someone_might_trust_it: Telecom security issues feel serious and time-sensitive.
recommended_action: End the call and contact the provider using an official number from the website or app.

title: Bank fraud team callback pressure
scenario_text: A caller says they are from the bank fraud team and asks the user to confirm a one-time code to stop a suspicious transaction.
why_someone_might_trust_it: The target wants to stop fraud quickly.
recommended_action: Do not share codes. Hang up and call the bank using the official number on the bank card or website.

title: IT support remote access request
scenario_text: Someone claiming to be from IT support says the user's device is infected and asks them to install remote-access software.
why_someone_might_trust_it: Users may assume IT can see problems before they do.
recommended_action: Refuse the request and verify with internal IT using official contact channels.

title: Family emergency money request by phone
scenario_text: A caller claims a relative is in trouble and pressures the user to transfer money immediately without calling anyone else.
recommended_action: Pause, independently contact the relative or another trusted family member, and do not send money until verified.

title: Legitimate payroll reminder
scenario_text: HR sends a scheduled reminder that payroll details can be reviewed through the company intranet. No links ask for passwords and no urgent action is demanded.
why_someone_might_trust_it: The message matches normal internal process.
recommended_action: Access the intranet through the usual internal route if needed.

title: Expected bank callback after user request
scenario_text: After the user contacts the bank through the official app, the bank returns the call and references the support case number without asking for passwords or one-time codes.
why_someone_might_trust_it: The callback was expected and follows a known support process.
recommended_action: Continue carefully and still avoid sharing passwords or MFA codes.

title: Known school notification
scenario_text: A school sends a routine newsletter from its normal domain with familiar branding and no requests for money, login details, or urgent confidential action.
why_someone_might_trust_it: It matches previous legitimate communication style.
recommended_action: Read normally and verify only if something unusual appears.

title: Internal IT maintenance notice
scenario_text: Internal IT announces planned maintenance and advises users that systems may be briefly unavailable. The message directs users to the internal service page rather than asking for login details.
why_someone_might_trust_it: It matches normal internal communication and change management.
recommended_action: Use the normal internal status page if you want to confirm the maintenance window.
